inline_comments.message: I'm not sure I'm understanding this method. Wouldn't you check if these keys are available first? Or maybe the preceding if statements don't make sense to me right now.
The previous calls return false if there is no data yet, so in that case, it falls into this block.  Nevertheless, I've re-arranged it to be less ambiguous.

It seems that the checks are weakened compared to the previous version.   The previous version I thought it would cover the case where DCAE sends onset.aai section with no prov-status information, so in turn will trigger an aai query (if no data is already present).   

That seems to me not to be the case now, so if dcae sends onset.aai with missing prov-status info (closed-loop-disabled info would be present in this scenario), that query won't happen anymore.  

Is the weakening of these checks intended?
Per discussion with Pam, we can assume if one flag is there, then both flags are there; it's either enriched completely with A&AI data, or it isn't enriched at all.
